summary: Gruber responds to Thom Holwerda's accusation that critics of Android's new Roboto font are biased 'font hipsters' who reject anything that isn't Helvetica. He argues the criticism is the opposite: Roboto is too derivative of Helvetica while being poorly executed. Gruber rejects the notion that design critics favor Apple blindly, pointing out he criticized Apple's own Marker Felt font on the iPhone's launch day. He praises Microsoft's Segoe font to demonstrate that good design is recognized regardless of platform. He concludes that criticizing Android's design isn't tribal loyalty but honest assessment, and that open source UIs have a long track record of being ugly.
key_points: The criticism of Roboto is not that it isn't Helvetica, but that it's a poor imitation — ungainly, homely, and unharmonious
Design critics judge Apple's choices harshly too, as demonstrated by widespread criticism of Marker Felt in iOS
Microsoft's Segoe font is universally praised by the same critics supposedly biased toward Apple, disproving tribal loyalty
The accusation that design critics are Apple partisans is a defensive construct by open source advocates who refuse to acknowledge that open source UIs tend to be ugly
Gruber selfishly wants Android to have a good system font because it renders his own site for Android readers
money_quotes: The criticism is simply that Roboto is ungainly, homely, unharmonious.
We love great design and beautiful things, and Apple tends to produce great design and beautiful things — we don't blindly love Apple and claim all it produces is great.
I dare you to find one critic of Roboto who claims to be a fan of Marker Felt.
No one from Holwerda's imaginary Apple-defending Helvetica-loving cabal has anything but praise for Segoe, as Metro's system font.
This idea that designers who favor iOS criticize Android for being poorly designed just because it's from an Apple competitor is nonsense.
We criticize Android for being poorly designed because it's poorly designed. We favor iOS because it's better designed. That's it.
A bogeyman construct dreamed up by open source zealots who refuse to believe over a decade of evidence that open source UIs tend to be ugly, and that ugly UIs tend to be unpopular.
key_insight: Criticizing Android's design choices isn't tribal loyalty to Apple — it's honest assessment from people who judge all platforms by the same design standards, as evidenced by their willingness to criticize Apple and praise Microsoft.
